HF.primaryRadarUse: Over-The-Horizon Radar (OTHR) exploiting ionospheric skywave propagation

VHF.primaryRadarUse: Long-range early warning; counter-stealth (resonance region for 2-10m objects)
VHF.stealthCounterCapability: VHF wavelengths (1-10m) approach resonance region for typical combat aircraft wingspans (10-15m). Radar Absorbing Material (RAM) optimized for X-band is largely ineffective at VHF.
disadvantages: Poor angular resolution (large beamwidth for aperture)

UHF.primaryRadarUse: Early warning, ballistic missile defense, space surveillance

L_Band.primaryRadarUse: Long-range air route surveillance, ATC, naval air search
advantages: Good balance of range and resolution
disadvantages: Larger than S-band for same gain

S_Band.primaryRadarUse: Medium-range air surveillance, naval volume search, weather radar
disadvantages: Attenuation in heavy rain (2-4 dB/km extreme)

X_Band.primaryRadarUse: Fire control, airborne FCR, naval fire control, SAR, weapon guidance
disadvantages: Significant rain attenuation (>3 dB/km in heavy rain)

Ku_Band.primaryRadarUse: Missile seekers, short-range surveillance, precision tracking, SHORAD

Ka_Band.primaryRadarUse: Millimeter wave precision seekers, drone detection, police speed radar

W_Band.primaryRadarUse: Very high resolution seekers, UAS detection, automotive radar, some counter-drone

PulsedUnmodulated.rdfs:label: Simple Pulsed (Unmodulated)
PulsedUnmodulated.description: Short rectangular pulse. Range resolution = c*τ/2 where τ is pulse width.
PulsedUnmodulated.limitation: Range-Doppler coupling; range resolution tied to pulse width (limits power)

LFM_Chirp.rdfs:label: Linear Frequency Modulated (LFM) Chirp
LFM_Chirp.description: Pulse where frequency sweeps linearly across bandwidth B during pulse width τ. Enables pulse compression: output range resolution = c/(2B), independent of τ.
LFM_Chirp.advantage: High average power (long pulse) with fine range resolution (large bandwidth). Most common modern radar waveform.
LFM_Chirp.peakSidelobe: First sidelobe at -13.3 dB (rectangular window); -40 dB with Taylor weighting

NLFM.rdfs:label: Nonlinear FM
NLFM.description: FM sweep with nonlinear rate; designed to have low range sidelobes without amplitude weighting loss.
NLFM.advantage: Low sidelobes (-35 to -45 dB) with no processing gain loss

FMCW.rdfs:label: Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW)
FMCW.description: Continuously transmits FM signal; range from beat frequency between TX and RX. No high-power pulsed transmitter required.

SteppedFrequency.rdfs:label: Stepped Frequency Waveform
SteppedFrequency.description: Sequence of pulses stepping across a wide bandwidth; coherent processing provides fine range resolution without instantaneous wide bandwidth hardware.
advantages: High range resolution without wide instantaneous bandwidth
disadvantages: Long coherent processing time; target must not move significantly across stepped bandwidth

OFDM.rdfs:label: OFDM Radar Waveform
OFDM.description: Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing adapted for radar; simultaneous transmission across multiple subcarriers enabling simultaneous range, Doppler, and angle measurement.

PseudoRandom.rdfs:label: Pseudo-Random / Phase-Coded Waveform
PseudoRandom.description: Phase-coded pulse sequences (Barker, Golay, Gold codes) or pseudo-random waveforms for LPI radar; difficult to detect or replicate by hostile ESM.
PseudoRandom.LPI_advantage: Wideband pseudo-random waveforms have low peak power spectral density, making them difficult to detect with narrowband receivers

PulseCompression.rdfs:label: Pulse Compression
PulseCompression.skos:definition: Matched filtering of LFM or phase-coded waveform to concentrate energy into narrow peak, achieving simultaneous high range resolution and high energy (SNR).
PulseCompression.outputs: Range-compressed signal with fine range resolution (c/2B)

FFT.rdfs:label: Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) / Doppler Processing
FFT.skos:definition: Coherent integration of N pulses via DFT/FFT across slow-time dimension. Creates range-Doppler map. Processing gain = 10*log10(N). Resolves targets by velocity.
FFT.slowTimeFFT_output: Range-Doppler matrix: rows = range gates, columns = Doppler bins (velocity)

MTI.rdfs:label: Moving Target Indicator (MTI)
MTI.skos:definition: Cancellation filter removing stationary or slow-moving clutter by subtracting successive returns. Works on blind velocities where target Doppler matches PRF alias.
MTI.blindVelocities: Targets with v = n * lambda * PRF / 2 (n=0,1,2...) are cancelled along with clutter
MTI.limitation: Blind velocity problem; overcome by staggered PRF

MTD.rdfs:label: Moving Target Detection (MTD)
MTD.skos:definition: Enhancement of MTI combining Doppler filtering (DFT bank) with adaptive CFAR. Modern standard for pulse-Doppler radars. Overcomes blind velocity problem.
MTD.processing: Doppler FFT bank following MTI canceller; each Doppler filter has its own CFAR threshold
MTD.improvement_over_MTI: No blind velocities (with staggered PRF); better clutter-to-noise ratio handling

CFAR.rdfs:label: Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR)
CFAR.skos:definition: Adaptive threshold detection algorithm maintaining constant probability of false alarm regardless of clutter level by estimating local noise/clutter power and setting threshold accordingly.

CA-CFAR.name: Cell-Averaging CFAR
CA-CFAR.description: Averages training cells around cell under test; optimal in Gaussian noise

GOCA-CFAR.name: Greatest-of CA-CFAR
GOCA-CFAR.description: Selects max of leading/lagging averages; better at clutter edges

SOCA-CFAR.name: Smallest-of CA-CFAR
SOCA-CFAR.description: Selects min; better in target-rich environments

OS-CFAR.name: Ordered Statistics CFAR
OS-CFAR.description: Ranks training cells; robust to interfering targets in reference window

VI-CFAR.name: Variability Index CFAR
VI-CFAR.description: Switches algorithm based on clutter statistics

AI-CFAR.name: AI-based CFAR
AI-CFAR.description: Deep learning CFAR adapting to complex non-stationary clutter; state of research

CFAR.applicationToSARDespeckling: CFAR detection in SAR images used for ship, vehicle, and change detection. Connected to StructDiff SAR-DDPM research: despeckling improves CFAR performance by reducing false alarms from speckle noise.

STAP.rdfs:label: Space-Time Adaptive Processing (STAP)
STAP.skos:definition: Joint spatial-temporal adaptive processing using multi-channel (multi-element) phased array data across multiple pulses to cancel airborne clutter which has Doppler spread dependent on antenna look angle (clutter ridge in STAP space).
STAP.mathematicalFormulation: Optimum STAP weight vector: w = R^{-1} * s / (s^H * R^{-1} * s), where R is the space-time clutter+noise covariance matrix and s is the target space-time steering vector.
STAP.requirement: Multiple receive channels (array); coherent multi-pulse processing
STAP.MDV_improvement_over_MTI: STAP can detect targets at 0.1-1 m/s vs MTI limit of ~5-10 m/s
STAP.computationalCost: O(N^2 * M^2) for full STAP; reduced-rank approximations required (JDL, EFA, PRI-staggered)

AdaptiveBeamforming.rdfs:label: Adaptive Beamforming / Digital Beamforming (DBF)
AdaptiveBeamforming.skos:definition: Real-time adaptation of antenna pattern weights to place nulls in the direction of jammers or interference while maintaining gain toward desired target.

algorithms.MVDR: Minimum Variance Distortionless Response; places deepest nulls but requires accurate signal direction
algorithms.LCMV: Linearly Constrained Minimum Variance; multiple simultaneous null constraints
algorithms.SMI: Sample Matrix Inversion; estimates covariance from received data
algorithms.DiagonalLoading: Regularization for poorly conditioned covariance matrix
algorithms.DeepLearning_ABF: CNN/RNN-based beamforming; handles coherent jammers and model mismatch

PulseDopplerProcessing.rdfs:label: Pulse-Doppler Processing
PulseDopplerProcessing.skos:definition: Coherent detection exploiting Doppler frequency shift for clutter rejection and target velocity measurement in airborne and high-velocity environments.

HighPRF.description: PRF chosen so maximum target Doppler is within first ambiguity; no Doppler ambiguities; range ambiguous
HighPRF.advantage: Unambiguous velocity; excellent clutter rejection in head-on geometry

MediumPRF.description: Compromise; both range and velocity ambiguous but manageable with multiple PRFs
MediumPRF.disadvantage: Requires multiple PRF sequences to resolve ambiguities

LowPRF.disadvantage: Poor clutter rejection for airborne platforms

KalmanFilter.description: Optimal linear estimator for Gaussian noise; predicts next state and updates with measurement. Standard for constant velocity / constant acceleration targets.
KalmanFilter.stateVector: Typically [x, y, z, vx, vy, vz] or extended with acceleration

EKF.description: Linearized Kalman for nonlinear measurement equations (radar measurements in polar coords; state in Cartesian)

UKF.description: Sigma-point Kalman filter; better nonlinear handling than EKF without linearization

ParticleFilter.description: Monte Carlo sequential importance sampling; handles arbitrary non-Gaussian distributions; computationally expensive but handles highly maneuvering targets and multimodal posteriors

IMM.description: Interacting Multiple Model estimator; bank of filters with different motion models weighted by likelihood; handles mode-switching maneuvers
IMM.advantage: Automatically switches between CV/CA/CT models; excellent for maneuvering targets

JPDA.description: Joint Probabilistic Data Association; handles measurement-to-track association uncertainty in cluttered environments
JPDA.application: Close formation tracking, dense target environments

MHT.description: Multiple Hypothesis Tracking; maintains multiple association hypotheses pruned over time; Bayesian optimal multi-target tracker
MHT.advantage: Handles track initiation/termination, crossing tracks, merged measurements
MHT.disadvantage: Exponential hypothesis growth; requires pruning (N-scan, k-best)

PHD_Filter.description: Probability Hypothesis Density filter (Random Finite Sets); propagates first moment of multi-target density; computationally efficient multi-target tracking

JetEngineModulation_JEM.description: Doppler modulation sidebands from rotating jet engine fan blades; unique signature per engine type

HelicopterBlade_Flash.description: Periodic returns from helicopter rotor blades; rate reveals rotor RPM; blade number reveals type

HighRangeResolutionProfile_HRRP.description: Range profile of target scattering centers at fine range resolution; matched against library

ISAR_Imaging.description: 2D radar image of target from relative motion; matched against 3D model library

MicroDoppler.description: Modulation of Doppler from rotating/vibrating sub-parts: helicopter blades, drone rotors, walking human limbs
MicroDoppler.application: UAS vs bird discrimination; human activity recognition; vehicle type

PolSAR_Classification.description: Polarimetric decomposition of SAR returns (Pauli, Cloude-Pottier H/A/alpha) for terrain and target classification
PolSAR_Classification.application: SAR image target classification; change detection; vehicle detection
PolSAR_Classification.connectionToStructDiff: Direct connection to SAR research; polarimetric features can enhance StructDiff change detection pipeline

AI_DeepLearning.description: CNN, RNN, Transformer-based classifiers trained on radar signature libraries
AI_DeepLearning.advantage: Outperforms model-based methods for complex targets and clutter

SpotJamming.description: High-power noise concentrated on exact radar frequency. Maximum J/S ratio but requires precise knowledge of radar frequency.
SpotJamming.advantage: Maximum jamming power density at exact frequency
SpotJamming.disadvantage: Ineffective if radar frequency hops; requires precise frequency knowledge

BarrageJamming.description: Noise spread across wide frequency band. Effective against frequency-agile radars but lower spectral power density per MHz.
BarrageJamming.J_S_penalty: Power spread across B_jammer/B_radar bandwidth ratio

SweptJamming.description: Noise swept rapidly across target frequency range; compromise between spot and barrage efficiency.

CoverPulseJamming.description: High-power pulse covering the radar receiver when range/angle truth would be measured. Used for range denial.

DRFM.rdfs:label: Digital Radio Frequency Memory (DRFM)
DRFM.skos:definition: Receives radar signal, stores digitally, and retransmits with modifications (time delay, Doppler shift, amplitude modulation) to create false target returns that appear coherent to the victim radar.
capabilities: DRFM repeater for SAM guidance denial
DRFM.fidelity: Modern DRFM can replicate radar signal with <1 ns timing accuracy and <0.1 Hz Doppler accuracy
DRFM.limitation: Coherent pulse compression radars can detect DRFM artifacts if processing is sophisticated
DRFM.counterCountermeasure: Randomized waveforms, high bandwidth LFM, polarization agility, cross-check across multiple radar

RangeGatePullOff_RGPO.description: DRFM repeater captures victim radar's range gate; generates delayed replica; slowly walks range gate away from truth target
RangeGatePullOff_RGPO.effectOnMissile: Missile seeker tracks false range gate; diverts from target

VelocityGatePullOff_VGPO.description: DRFM captures Doppler gate; slowly Doppler-shifts false return away from truth
VelocityGatePullOff_VGPO.effectOnMissile: Missile notch filter tracks false Doppler; target appears stationary (clutter) and is rejected

FalseTargetGeneration.description: DRFM generates multiple simultaneous false targets with varied range, Doppler, and angle to saturate radar tracking and fire control
FalseTargetGeneration.saturationTarget: Modern DRFM can generate 50-200 simultaneous false targets

CrossEye.description: Two-element jammer with controlled phase difference creates a glint-like angular error in monopulse angle measurement
CrossEye.effectOnMonopulse: Monopulse angle error proportional to separation between cross-eye elements and amplitude/phase control

Chaff.description: Dipole reflectors (typically cut aluminum foil or metalized glass fiber) dispensed in cloud; each dipole resonant at λ/2 of threat radar frequency
Chaff.disadvantage: Wind-dependent; known Doppler (zero radial velocity if not deployed from moving platform); modern radars discriminate by Doppler

AirborneDecoys.description: Powered or unpowered decoys mimicking aircraft radar signature. Active decoys include DRFM to enhance and modify returns.

ADM-160_MALD.type: Miniature Air Launched Decoy; active RF emitter

BriteCloud.note: DRFM in tube dispensed from chaff/flare dispenser

InflatableDecoys.description: Corner reflectors or inflatable structures mimicking large vehicles or aircraft for deception of SAR and ground surveillance radars

AntiRadiationMissile_ARM.rdfs:label: Anti-Radiation Missile (ARM)
AntiRadiationMissile_ARM.skos:definition: Missile homing passively on radar emissions. Primary kinetic threat to emitting radars.
AntiRadiationMissile_ARM.guidanceMode: Passive anti-radiation homing on radar RF emissions

ALARM.feature: Loitering mode; parachutes then re-engages when radar re-activates

radarCountermeasures: Dispersed radar network (no single emitter target)

ECCM.rdfs:label: Electronic Counter-Countermeasures (ECCM)
ECCM.skos:definition: Radar design features and operational techniques that reduce susceptibility to jamming, deception, and ARM attack.

FrequencyAgility.description: Pulse-to-pulse or burst-to-burst frequency hopping across wide band. Forces barrage jamming or prevents spot jamming.
FrequencyAgility.effectiveness: Reduces DRFM effectiveness; forces barrage jamming (lower J/S)

PulseCompression.ECCMbenefit: High time-bandwidth product waveform raises threshold for coherent DRFM jamming; random waveforms prevent DRFM coherent replication

LowSidelobeLSL.description: Ultra-low sidelobe antenna (-45 to -55 dB PSLL) prevents jamming entering through sidelobes
LowSidelobeLSL.designComplexity: Requires precise amplitude tapering; manufacturing tolerance critical

SidelobleCancellation_SLC.description: Auxiliary antennas adaptively null jammers entering sidelobes of main antenna

HomeOnJam_HOJ.description: Missile guidance mode using jammer emissions as beacon when SARH illumination fails due to jamming

BurnThrough.description: Operating at maximum power in reduced-coverage sector to overcome noise jamming; effective when J/S is marginal
BurnThrough.burnThrough_range_typical: Radar detection range in jamming = normal range / sqrt(J/S + 1)

LPI.rdfs:label: Low Probability of Intercept (LPI)
LPI.description: Radar design minimizing detectability by hostile ESM/RWR receivers through combination of low peak power, wide bandwidth, pseudo-random waveforms, and agile frequency/PRF

CognitiveECCM.description: AI/ML-based real-time ECCM: detect jamming type, select optimal counter, adapt waveform autonomously

techniques: Multiple simultaneous diverse waveforms per beam

SilentMode.description: Radar ceases emission when ARM attack detected (via RWR); relies on netted radar from different location to maintain track

ARM_Attack.mitigation: Silent mode, frequency agility, ARM-detecting RWR, dispersal

EMP.mitigation: Hardening, Faraday shielding, surge protection, offline storage

DeceptionJamming.mitigation: Waveform diversity, coherent processing, cross-check multiple sensors

Spoofing_GPS.mitigation: Multi-constellation GPS, INS backup, anti-spoof receivers, M-code

CyberAttack.mitigation: Air gap, hardened OS, TEMPEST shielding, secure supply chain, regular patching

SupplyChainCompromise.mitigation: Trusted supplier chain, chip-level verification, hardware trojan detection

WeatherDegradation.mitigation: Environmental hardening, lower frequency selection, adaptive waveform

StealthTargets.description: Low-observable targets with RCS < 0.01 m² require significant additional transmitter power or specialized processing
StealthTargets.mitigation: Bistatic radar, VHF/UHF (resonance), multistatic netted radar, long dwell time, ISAR, very large aperture

DroneSwarm.description: Large numbers of small, cheap drones with very low RCS; overwhelming sensor capacity and fire control
DroneSwarm.mitigation: AI-based detection, high PRF, mmW radar, sensor fusion, networked C-UAS
